channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Ohio colleges could be banned from asking student pronouns on applications
item.description: Ohio's public universities could be banned from asking prospective students their preferred pronouns on applications.
item.dc:creator: David Rees, NewsNation

item.title: Trump names Brendan Carr, senior GOP leader at FCC, to lead the agency
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has named the senior Republican on the Federal Communications Commission as the new chairman of the agency tasked with regulating broadcasting, telecommunications and broadband.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert And Colleen Long, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pollster Ann Selzer steps aside in wake of huge Iowa poll miss
item.description: Just before the election, a poll from Selzer sent shockwaves nationwide after it found Vice President Harris leading President-elect Trump in Iowa.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Former Trump WH COVID doc 'excited' for questions at RFK confirmation hearing
item.description: Dr. Deborah Birx said she is "excited" for the opportunity for a transparent discussion at Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s Senate confirmation hearing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Big voter turnout this year benefited Republicans, contradicting conventional political wisdom
item.description: The 2024 presidential election featured sky-high turnout, approaching the historic levels of the 2020 contest and contradicting long-held conventional political wisdom that Republicans struggle to win races in which many people vote.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota election judge faces felony charges over accepting unregistered votes
item.description: An election judge in the state of Minnesota is facing felony charges after allegedly allowing 11 people to vote who did not have the proper documentation on Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democratic AGs rush to form line of defense against Trump
item.description: Democratic attorneys general across the country are readying their legal defenses against the incoming Trump administration, preparing to pounce on potential violations and even take the president-elect to court if he implements controversial policies.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard's sympathetic views toward Russia cause alarm as Trump's pick to lead intelligence services
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Tulsi Gabbard, President-elect Donald Trump's choice to lead the U.S. intelligence services, in 2022 endorsed one of Russia's main justifications for invading Ukraine: the existence of dozens of U.S.-funded biolabs working on some of the world's nastiest pathogens.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, ELLEN KNICKMEYER and EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump allies remain in legal peril despite his election victory
item.description: President-elect Trump’s White House victory set his legal cases on a downward spiral, but his allies who have faced prosecution or other legal trouble are not yet in the clear.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden and Xi take a first step to limit AI and nuclear decisions at their last meeting
item.description: The leaders agreed that humans should control decisions to use nuclear weapons — not AI. And they talked about the importance of stability during the transition to a new U.S. administration.
item.dc:creator: Asma Khalid, MPR News

item.title: Here are the people Trump has picked for key positions so far
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is filling key posts in his second administration, and it's shaping up much differently than his first. He's prioritizing loyalists for top jobs. Trump was bruised and hampered by internal squabbles during his initial term in office.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump names fossil fuel executive Chris Wright as energy secretary
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has selected Chris Wright, a campaign donor and fossil fuel executive, to serve as Energy secretary after his second administration begins in January.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump names attorney Will Scharf to key White House job
item.description: Scharf represented Trump as one of his lawyers during a hearing on presidential immunity before the Supreme Court and was a regular on television defending Trump amid his hush money trial in Manhattan.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: California slated to rename places to remove Native American slur
item.description: The agency was ordered to rename streets, bridges, public buildings, cemeteries and other places that use the slur.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Lara Trump emerges as an early favorite to fill Rubio's Senate seat
item.description: Appointing Lara Trump would ensure that the incoming president has a loyalist in the Senate, but it would most definitely come with criticism from Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Kamala Harris raised $1 billion-plus in defeat. She's still sending persistent appeals to donors
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Kamala Harris and the Democratic Party's prodigious fundraising operation raised more than $1 billion in her loss to Donald Trump, but the vice president is still pushing donors for more money after the election.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nicholas Begich projected to win Alaska US House-at-large race
item.description: The top four candidates on the general election ballot included a Democrat serving a 20-year prison sentence in New York.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Trump is creating team of loyalists after conflicts with Cabinet members in 1st term
item.description: In his second term, President-elect Donald Trump is surrounding himself with people who can amplify his message and agree with how he wants to remake the executive branch.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Iranian official met with Musk in a possible step to ease tensions with Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Iran successfully sought a meeting with Elon Musk, according to a U.S. official, one in a series of steps that appeared aimed at easing tensions with President-elect Donald Trump. Iran's U.N.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump names Karoline Leavitt as youngest ever White House press secretary
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Friday named Karoline Leavitt, his campaign press secretary, to serve as his White House press secretary. Leavitt, 27, currently a spokesperson for Trump's transition, would be the youngest White House press secretary in history.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hubbard County election judge faces felony charges for allegedly allowing unregistered voters to cast ballots
item.description: Prosecutors in Hubbard County say Timothy Michael Scouton — who was head judge at a precinct in rural Badoura Township — allowed 11 people to vote even though they had not registered.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: Police: Trump cabinet pick Hegseth involved in 2017 sexual assault investigation
item.description: Hegseth, who Donald Trump recently tapped to lead the Department of Defense, was investigated for sexual assault in 2017, the Monterey Police Department said.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, NewsNation

item.title: Ryan Walters filmed himself praying for Trump, made students watch it
item.description: Walters also announced Thursday the purchase of more than 500 Bibles for use in advanced placement government classes.
item.dc:creator: Katie Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Trump picks former Georgia congressman for VA secretary
item.description: President-elect Trump has announced former Rep. Doug Collins (R-Ga.), a chaplain in the Air Force Reserve, as his pick for the next Secretary of Veterans Affairs.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: NJ Transit, Amtrak testify before New Jersey lawmakers
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) - Some palpable tension could be felt during Thursday's Assembly Transportation and Independent Authorities (TIA) Committee, which featured NJ Transit President Kevin Corbett and Amtrak CEO Stephen Gardner.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Florida GOP chair approves Gaetz AG nomination; state democratic chair concerned
item.description: There are increasing questions from political figures surrounding President-elect Donald Trump’s pick to lead the Justice Department. However, former Rep. Matt Gaetz is getting support from Evan Power, the chair of the Florida GOP.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gaetz once faced sex trafficking investigation by Justice Dept. he could now lead
item.description: Inside a private meeting of House Republicans, the news that Trump had chosen Gaetz to lead the Justice Department sent a shock.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Thousands of votes never counted across Nevada due to signature issues
item.description: More than 9,000 mail-in ballots across Nevada were not counted because voters did not verify their signatures before the curing deadline, according to data the 8 News Now Investigators reviewed.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Many Californians would back Kamala Harris for California governor, new poll suggests
item.description: It’s unclear what Vice President Kamala Harris will do after losing the presidential election, but should she run for California governor, support among state residents is seemingly strong, new polling suggests.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Cannizzaro, Yeager to continue leadership roles in Nevada Senate, Assembly
item.description: Democratic Nevada State Senator Nicole Cannizzaro will serve for a fourth consecutive legislative session as Senate Majority Leader. Democrat Assembly Speaker Steve Yeager was also reelected.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Bleakley, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Thune elected Senate majority leader
item.description: Sen. John Thune (R-S.D.) has been elected Senate majority leader, setting the stage for him to replace retiring Sen. Mitch McConnell (Ky.), who has held the top Senate GOP leadership job for the past 18 years. Thune has served as Senate Republican whip, the No.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WGN 9

item.title: Elon Musk, Vivek Ramaswamy land Trump administration roles
item.description: President-elect Trump has tapped tech entrepreneurs Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy to lead an advisory group focused on cutting federal spending.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's picks so far: Here's who will be advising the new president
item.description: Donald Trump has begun selecting people to fill top administration jobs in Washington upon returning to the Oval Office in January. Trump beat Vice President Kamala Harris in the 2024 election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump tapping Noem to lead Homeland Security: Reports
item.description: Noem was first floated as a possible running mate for Trump, but her consideration fizzled after the release of her biography, in which she described killing a dog that was “untrainable.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, WGN 9
